title: Cooter

jokes.text: What are you in charge of exactly? We're sharing the load. It's a bit of Homeland Security... We still have that?
jokes.setup: Question about Jack's new government responsibilities
jokes.punchline: Casual uncertainty about whether Homeland Security still exists
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire delivered with perfect deadpan uncertainty. The parenthetical question lands because it's both absurd and believable.

jokes.text: ...extreme weather preparedness, and the War on the Poor. You mean the War on Poverty. Yeah, okay, let's go with that.
jokes.punchline: Casual correction from 'War on the Poor' to 'War on Poverty' with dismissive agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy - the casual slip reveals underlying attitudes. Jack's dismissive 'let's go with that' makes it even funnier. Excellent character work.

jokes.text: The cryogenecists are already sharpening their head saws.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Geiss potentially coming out of coma
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of preparing to freeze his head
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd corporate world-building. The specificity of 'sharpening their head saws' sells the joke completely.

jokes.text: Hey, don't knock my Sabor de Soledad. I found a prize in here the other day... I hope.
jokes.setup: Liz defending her Mexican cheese curls
jokes.punchline: Uncertainty about whether the 'prize' was actually a prize
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's questionable food choices. The hesitant 'I hope' adds perfect uncertainty.

jokes.text: Liz Lemon, do these look like wizard nipples to you?
jokes.punchline: Completely unexpected question about wizard anatomy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cold open to a scene. The specificity and complete randomness make it perfect Tracy dialogue. Instantly quotable.

jokes.text: Well, I don't know. Are the lightning bolts supposed to be going in or out?
jokes.punchline: Liz treating it as a serious design question
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response. Liz's willingness to engage seriously with Tracy's insanity is classic character work.

jokes.text: The working title is 'Goregasm... colon... The Legend of Dong-Slayer.'
jokes.setup: Liz asking about the porn video game
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously over-the-top title with formal punctuation note
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tracy absurdity. The formal 'colon' pronunciation adds extra comedy to the already ridiculous title.

jokes.text: Yeah, you fixulate this!
jokes.setup: Liz apologizing about the wizard issue
jokes.punchline: Tracy's made-up word 'fixulate'
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy malapropism. Shows his character through language invention, though not as strong as his best moments.

jokes.text: Uh-oh. Emotions. You having your woman times?
jokes.punchline: Tracy's incredibly inappropriate and childish euphemism
jokes.explanation: Good character work for Tracy's immaturity, though the concept is fairly predictable. 'Woman times' is appropriately awkward.

jokes.text: Why don't I cross off the days like people in the movies?
jokes.setup: Liz realizing she might be late on her period
jokes.punchline: Self-aware reference to movie tropes about tracking time
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment about movie conventions. Works for Liz's analytical nature but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: I think you're supposed to take all of them, like, in a row.
jokes.setup: Context of Liz looking at birth control pills
jokes.punchline: Cerie's helpful but obvious advice about how pills work
jokes.explanation: Good Cerie character moment - helpful but stating the obvious. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: But I have been sexually active since I was 25.
jokes.punchline: Liz's late start to sexual activity revealed casually
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment. The casual delivery of what should be embarrassing information is very her.

jokes.text: Bad news, archers. President Carter has decided to boycott the Moscow Games. Peanut farmer.
jokes.setup: Kenneth talking about almost going to Olympics
jokes.punchline: Dismissive 'peanut farmer' insult about Carter
jokes.explanation: Great Kenneth moment showing his mysterious past and casual historical knowledge. 'Peanut farmer' as an insult is perfect.

jokes.text: Peanut farmer.
jokes.punchline: Dismissive two-word summary of former president
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing. Pete's bitter reduction of Carter to his pre-political career.

jokes.text: Saboteur!
jokes.setup: Realizing someone gave him wrong information about deadline
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's dramatic accusation
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth dramatic moment. The single word accusation fits his earnest nature perfectly.

jokes.text: Donny? Parcell. I thought you'd be coming out of that door.
jokes.punchline: Implication they were both hiding/waiting for each other
jokes.explanation: Setup for their rivalry subplot, functional but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: I don't like to think of this President as a lame duck. I think of him as a lame eagle.
jokes.punchline: Upgrading 'lame duck' to 'lame eagle' for patriotism
jokes.explanation: Clever political wordplay that perfectly establishes Cooter's character. The logic is both absurd and somehow patriotic.

jokes.text: The ceiling appears to be leaking. No, it's not. We've looked into it, and it's not.
jokes.punchline: Cooter's emphatic denial of visible reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect government incompetence satire. The insistence that they've 'looked into it' makes the denial even funnier.

jokes.text: Do you need a pen? Nope, I've kind of gotten used to it.
jokes.setup: Cooter offering to write down his extension
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of not having basic office supplies
jokes.explanation: Good government dysfunction joke. Cooter's matter-of-fact acceptance of the absurd situation works well.

jokes.text: We're not in a recession.
jokes.setup: Jack questioning lack of basic supplies
jokes.punchline: Cooter's defensive denial of economic problems
jokes.explanation: Solid political satire moment. The defensive tone while lacking pens perfectly captures government dysfunction.

jokes.text: I know, I'm not drunk, either, but we'll manage.
jokes.setup: Cooter saying he's not prepared for meeting
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that being drunk would be normal preparation
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy about government dysfunction. The casual tone makes the alcoholism implication even funnier.

jokes.text: Mother!
jokes.setup: Liz entering Jack's office and realizing he's not there
jokes.punchline: Exclamation suggesting maternal neediness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's dependence on Jack. Simple but effective revelation of her emotional state.

jokes.text: Not you, Grizz. You're doing great. I memorized all my lines at home.
jokes.punchline: Grizz's earnest professionalism in porn game voice acting
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment. Grizz's earnest professionalism in this ridiculous context is very funny.

jokes.text: I don't need another Judi Dench situation.
jokes.punchline: Reference to having problems with prestigious actress Judi Dench
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy moment. The idea that he's worked with Judi Dench and had problems is perfectly absurd for his character.

jokes.text: Touch my knees-butt.
jokes.setup: Tracy directing voice acting for game options
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up body part 'knees-butt'
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy absurdity. 'Knees-butt' is perfectly stupid and quotable, though the setup is a bit loose.

jokes.text: I can't support that. 'Dam' is a swear word.
jokes.punchline: Refusing support because 'dam' sounds like 'damn'
jokes.explanation: Great government stupidity satire. The bureaucratic logic applied to homophone concerns is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I'd support it if instead of dam, we called it a God finger.
jokes.punchline: Suggesting 'God finger' as alternative
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the dam joke. 'God finger' is both religiously appropriate and completely ridiculous.

jokes.text: Well, that's one theory.
jokes.setup: Jack saying Rome wasn't built in a day
jokes.punchline: Treating historical fact as debatable theory
jokes.explanation: Nice understated government incompetence joke. Questioning basic historical facts fits the bureaucratic mindset.

jokes.text: We need hope. We need change. We need experience. We need pens.
jokes.punchline: Building to the mundane need for basic office supplies
jokes.explanation: Excellent political speech parody with perfect escalation structure. The bathos from lofty ideals to 'pens' is masterful.

jokes.text: Did someone say 'Donny'? No. No. Oh. It's pretty muffled in there.
jokes.punchline: Donny mishearing from inside something, admitting poor acoustics
jokes.explanation: Solid running gag moment. Donny's matter-of-fact admission about the muffled sound adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: That's the only thing I know how to say in Chinese. 41/2 hours, Hee-Haw.
jokes.punchline: Donny's only Chinese phrase and rural nickname for Kenneth
jokes.explanation: Good character work for Donny. The random Chinese phrase and 'Hee-Haw' nickname fit his antagonistic nature.

jokes.text: It's hard for me to watch 'American Idol,' because I have perfect pitch. Oh. Ew.
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's disgusted reaction to the concept
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about humble-bragging. Jenna's example and Kenneth's moral disgust work well together.

jokes.text: It's hard for me to watch 'American Idol' 'cause there's a water bug on my channel changer.
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to do a back-door brag
jokes.punchline: Completely missing the point with genuine problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment. His inability to brag and genuine water bug problem completely subvert the lesson.

jokes.text: I haven't felt this energized at work since the two weeks when they tried to teach us Farsi.
jokes.setup: Cooter expressing excitement about Jack's presence
jokes.punchline: Reference to brief, failed government language program
jokes.explanation: Great government incompetence detail. The specificity of 'two weeks' and 'Farsi' makes it perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Jackie Boy.
jokes.punchline: Using Jack's nickname, calling him back to GE
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic callback that drives plot. The nickname establishes the relationship importance effectively.

jokes.text: He brings good things to light!
jokes.setup: Jack explaining importance of GE head
jokes.punchline: Using GE's actual slogan as patriotic rallying cry
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jack moment using corporate slogan as personal mission. Perfect character integration of his GE loyalty.

jokes.text: Hey, the pens are here!
jokes.setup: Jack trying to resign after passionate speech about pens
jokes.punchline: Pens arriving just as he's leaving
jokes.explanation: Good ironic timing joke. The pens arriving as Jack leaves adds to the government dysfunction theme.

jokes.text: U.S.A!
jokes.punchline: Patriotic chant over basic office supplies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of pen celebration. The patriotic fervor over mundane supplies fits the government satire.

jokes.text: As my mom used to say, 'You never want this to happen.'
jokes.setup: Trying to comfort Liz about pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Mother's unhelpful life advice
jokes.explanation: Good Cerie family background joke. The vague, unhelpful maternal wisdom fits her character perfectly.

jokes.text: You had a three-way with Roseanne and Tom Arnold.
jokes.setup: Liz saying Jenna makes sex mistakes too
jokes.punchline: Specific, horrifying example
jokes.explanation: Excellent shocking revelation. The specificity and grossness make it memorable, though edges toward cheap shock.

jokes.text: That was two years ago.
jokes.setup: Liz's shocked reaction to the three-way revelation
jokes.punchline: Jenna's defensive response about timing, not content
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that makes the original joke even better. Jenna defending the timing not the act is classic.

jokes.text: like Erin Brockovich or Sarah Connor.
jokes.setup: Liz saying she'll be a kick-ass single mom
jokes.punchline: Comparing herself to fictional/unlikely role models
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's pop culture-influenced thinking. The comparison works for her personality.

jokes.text: They're written in ketchup, dirty rock, leak water.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate writing materials
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing government desperation. 'Dirty rock' and 'leak water' are perfectly specific and absurd.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! They're caps, nothing but caps!
jokes.punchline: Devastating discovery they're incomplete
jokes.explanation: Perfect government incompetence escalation. The dramatic delivery of 'nothing but caps!' is beautifully absurd.

jokes.text: That's not my name! My name is James Reilly! Cooter Berger? What do you think I am, a cartoon dog?
jokes.setup: Breaking point over the pen caps
jokes.punchline: Revealing his real name and insulting nickname origin
jokes.explanation: Excellent character revelation. 'Cartoon dog' is a perfect description of how absurd his nickname is.

jokes.text: Cooter because I look like a turtle and Berger because he saw me eating a hamburger one time.
jokes.punchline: Completely arbitrary presidential naming logic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist government satire. The casual presidential nicknaming system is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: It wasn't even a hamburger. It was a sandwich.
jokes.punchline: Correcting the record on an already absurd detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation. Being precise about the sandwich detail makes the absurdity even better.

jokes.text: Pre... before. Natal... ruined.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining he knows what prenatal means
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong etymology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis stupidity moment. The confident wrong etymology is exactly his character.

jokes.text: if it's a boy, we're gonna name him Morpheus like that guy in 'The Matrix.' If it's a girl... I used to boff this chick named Judy, and I would love to honor her.
jokes.punchline: Wanting to name daughter after sexual conquest
jokes.explanation: Good Dennis character moment showing his complete inappropriateness and poor judgment.

jokes.text: Morpheus hears everything that you say.
jokes.setup: Liz telling Dennis to get out
jokes.punchline: Dennis acting like fetus can hear and understand
jokes.explanation: Good Dennis absurdity. The idea that the fetus is already 'Morpheus' and listening is appropriately stupid.

jokes.text: You must have been such a pretty monkey.
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to an attractive primate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna backhanded compliment. Calling someone a 'pretty monkey' is exactly her brand of insult.

jokes.text: You must have been such a pretty monkey.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre compliment comparing Kenneth to attractive primate
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully strange Jenna compliment. The backhanded nature of calling someone a 'pretty monkey' is perfect.

jokes.text: a nonlethal chemical weapon that would, 'reduce enemy soldiers' combat posture by making them totally gaybones for each other.'
jokes.punchline: Official military language for 'gay bomb' including 'gaybones'
jokes.explanation: Great military bureaucracy satire. The formal language combined with 'gaybones' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Like eating a burrito before sex.
jokes.punchline: Specific, relatable example of poor timing
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor. The specificity makes it relatable while staying appropriately crude.

jokes.text: Run, Kenneth, run!
jokes.punchline: Crowd cheering him on like a sports hero
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy moment. Works for the scene but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: I did certain things for you in bed that you were going to reciprocate. But then we broke up before my birthday, so you owe me.
jokes.punchline: Treating sexual favors as debt to be repaid
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy about transactional relationships. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: No crying in my bath tonight.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of regular bath crying
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Jack's hidden vulnerability. The casual tone makes it perfect.

jokes.text: Apparently, Sabor de Soledad gets its special tangy flavor from evaporated bull semen.
jokes.punchline: Disgusting ingredient revelation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback payoff. The gross-out factor combined with the setup makes this extremely memorable.

jokes.text: Oh, that explains your hair's thickness and shine.
jokes.punchline: Jack finding positive aspect of consumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack response. Finding the silver lining in bull semen consumption is exactly his character.

jokes.text: The Duffy men use those like ATMs.
jokes.punchline: Implying Dennis's family frequently donates sperm
jokes.explanation: Excellent Duffy family characterization. The ATM analogy is both crude and perfectly specific.

jokes.text: The President gave me a nickname. 'The Jacker.'
jokes.punchline: Presidential nickname with unfortunate implications
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the presidential naming theme. 'The Jacker' has obvious double meaning Jack doesn't recognize.

jokes.text: I feel weird. Let's do this.
jokes.setup: Gay bomb being deployed in enclosed space
jokes.punchline: Officials beginning to feel effects
jokes.explanation: Decent callback payoff but fairly predictable. Works as story conclusion but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Frank, you've been in your office for three months. What?! Yes! I'm gonna be a billionaire!
jokes.setup: Frank saying he played the game for a couple hours
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's been playing for months without realizing
jokes.explanation: Great time distortion comedy. Frank's complete loss of time awareness and immediate excitement about money is perfect.

tmdb_overview: In the second season finale, Jack moves on to a new job involving politics in Washington, but the position is not quite what he anticipated. Meanwhile, Liz suffers a pregnancy scare, Kenneth confronts rival page Donny when applying for a summer job, and Tracy works on his latest invention.
insight: Elite density carries absurdist escalation: 63 jokes in 22 minutes sustains 94+ rating.
